meaning: adj.内向的,含蓄的,闭关自守的
examples: Machen was a lonely, introverted child.
Inherently introverted, he realised early on that as an academic, his reticence would prove disastrous in the lecture hall.
I used to be incredibly introverted.
Introverted, ineffective communicators have learned to open up and take the stage with confidence and enthusiasm.
The gregarious and moody McColl was the opposite of the introverted and steady Lewis.
